data.title: Tesla, Palantir, Alphabet Surge as AI Buzz Lifts Tech Giants
data.description:
Aug 8 - Tesla (TSLA, Financial) and Palantir Technologies led mega-cap gains on Friday as broader markets advanced, buoyed by AI developments and investor positioning around Federal Reserve leadership shifts, according to a Friday press release.

Palantir rose nearly 3% after Unstructured joined its FedStart program, designed to help software firms meet federal compliance faster. Tesla added more than 3% amid ongoing AI sector strength. Alphabet also gained over 2%.

Large-cap stocks saw sharp moves as well. Gilead Sciences jumped 8% following a Buy rating upgrade from Truist Securities on HIV treatment momentum, while Natera surged 14% on a revenue beat. Doximity (DOCS), GenOn Energy (GEN), and Sotera Health (SHC) rose between 11% to 20%.

On the downside, The Trade Desk plunged 38% after a Sell rating from MoffettNathanson on valuation concerns. Twilio sank 17%, while Under Armour , Sweetgreen , and Sezzle all posted double-digit losses.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Settle Under $3 — Market Talk
data.description: U.S. natural gas's struggle around $3/mmBtu continues with concerns about ample storage and production countering the rise in LNG feedgas flows and a near-term pickup in weather-driven demand. "Currently weather patterns are plenty hot enough the next 15 days, although the risk is the weather data loses a few CDDs [cooling degree days] in time," NatGasWeather.com says in a note. "Whether prices trade over or under $3 at the Sunday reopen is likely to be dependent on the direction of weekend weather trends." Nymex natural gas settles down 2.5% at $2.99/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: Soybean Futures Fall as Analysts Mull WASDE Possibilities — Daily Grain Highlights
data.description:
By Kirk Maltais

  • Soybeans for November delivery fell 0.7% to $9.86 1/4 a bushel on the Chicago Board of Trade on Friday, with Tuesday's WASDE report from the USDA potentially showing a significant lift in yield projections.
  • Wheat for September delivery fell 0.5% to $5.15 3/4 a bushel.
  • Corn for December delivery fell 0.4% to $4.05 1/2 a bushel.

HIGHLIGHTS

Trending Higher: Grain traders spent the day looking ahead to next week's WASDE report.

Yields and production are expected to rise from the previous month, with analysts debating how much they climb.

Soybean yields may show the biggest uptick, said Karen Braun of Zaner Ag Hedge.

"If I had to vote for a potential yield surprise scenario this year, it would be soybean yield to the high side," she said.

She points out that the USDA has raised its soybean yield forecast in its August report from the prior month in eight of the past 10 years, with the largest of those upticks being an increase of 7% in 2020.

Passing Through: A cold front passing through the Corn Belt this weekend will serve to keep the weather favorable for growing row crops over the weekend, the USDA says.

"A cold front crossing the Plains and Midwest, as well as a high-pressure system over the Northeast, will help to cap the northward extent of heat expanding from the Southwest," the USDA said. "Consequently, Midwestern temperatures should remain mostly favorable for summer crops, including corn and soybeans, for the next several days." Rainfall is also expected for these areas.

INSIGHT

Crop Cut: Black Sea consultancy SovEcon has cut its projection for the Russian wheat crop, reducing it by 300,000 metric tons to 83.3 million tons.

While it is a reduction in crop size, it is still up 700,000 tons from Russia's production last year, according to the firm.

A degradation of crop conditions in the southern part of the country has impacted the firm's outlook, SovEcon said in a note Friday, with lower yields cutting production in the south by 900,000 tons.

"As of late August, harvesting in the south is nearing completion, making a recovery in yields unlikely," said SovEcon.

South American Share: Chinese data are showing that the country imported 11.7 million metric tons of soybeans in July, up 18% from this time last year.

Overall, 61 million tons of soybeans have been imported in the first seven months of the year, up 4.6% from the same timeframe last year.

While data for the origins of the soybeans aren't yet available for July, in June 87% of the soybeans imported by China came from Brazil.

With the current trade climate, U.S. sales may stay under wraps through the rest of the year, said Commerzbank in a note.

Spare No Expense: World food prices rose last month, driven by meat and vegetable oils despite declines in cereal, dairy and sugar prices, the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations said.

The FAO's food price index, which tracks global prices for a basket of staple foods, averaged 130.1 points in July, a 1.6% increase from June and 7.6% higher than a year earlier. The index remains 18.8% below its peak reached in March 2022 after Russia's invasion of Ukraine.

Meat prices rose 1.2% to a record high, supported by strong U.S. and Chinese import demand for bovine and ovine meats. Vegetable-oil prices surged 7.1% from the previous month on palm, soy and sunflower oils, reaching a three-year high.

AHEAD

  • The USDA is scheduled to release its weekly grain export inspections report at 11 a.m. EDT Monday.
  • The USDA is due to release its weekly crop progress report at 4 p.m. EDT Monday.
  • Smithfield Foods Inc. is scheduled to release its 2Q earnings before the stock market opens Tuesday.
  • The USDA is due to release its monthly WASDE report at noon EDT Tuesday.
  • Giulia Petroni contributed to this article.

Write to Kirk Maltais at kirk.maltais@wsj.com

data.title: This Pizza Chain's Stock Surged 360%--But Is the Slice Still Worth the Price?
data.description:
Once a turnaround story, Casey's has quietly become one of the market's most impressive retail compounders. Since 2018, shares have climbed more than 360% as the company revamped its strategy under CEO Darren Rebelezcentralizing procurement, streamlining operations, and betting big on fresh food. That bet's paid off. Prepared food now contributes nearly a third of gross profit, well above peers, while private-label items are benefiting from the company's strong reputation. Bloomberg Second Measure data shows Casey's outpacing the broader staples sector in sales growthdriven by pizza, donuts, breakfast burritos, and even a trial menu of wings and fries.

But it's not just food that's fueling the run. Casey's has leaned into M&A, targeting small chains that make up over 60% of the 150,000+ U.S. convenience stores. Its biggest deal yetlast year's $1.1 billion acquisition of CEFCOadded nearly 200 stores and a key fuel terminal, extending Casey's reach into high-growth states like Texas and Florida. The company has been adding around 100 stores a year, with a current footprint of about 2,900 locations across 19 states. That's still far behind giants like 7-Eleven or Circle K, but Casey's rural focus gives it pricing power and customer loyalty in markets with limited competition.

Still, not every analyst is on board at these levels. The stock closed at $518.28 on Thursday, trading at roughly 35x earningsa premium to convenience store peers and even most pizza chains. BMO's Kelly Bania noted that rising expectations leave little margin for error, while Wells Fargo's Anthony Bonadio flagged that any slowdown in growthor signs of strength from other retailerscould trigger a rotation. Others remain constructive, pointing to Casey's unique traffic drivers: fuel, beer, tobacco, and foodan edge that traditional QSRs can't match. But with the bar set this high, Casey's next chapter will need more than just good pizza.

data.title: During Lazy, Hazy Summer Days, Advisors Find New Ways to Connect With Clients — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Sally Cates

Summer may signal a slowdown for some industries, but for many financial advisors, it simply means adjusting the rhythm. Whether that entails hopping on planes to meet clients at their second homes, cruising along the coast of Maine with lobster rolls in tow, or staying digitally connected from afar, top advisors find creative ways to maintain relationships without disrupting the season's slower, more relaxed pace.

We asked three advisors how they are navigating client communications, referrals, and relationship-building during the summer months. Their insights show that meaningful engagement doesn't take a vacation.

Elizabeth (Lisa) van Walleghem, CEO and founder, Maximai Investment Partners, Coral Gables Fla.

We're based in Coral Gables, Fla., and for our U.S. clients, we do travel to their vacation homes, as I did in early June. We also visit them in their primary residences from time to time. And we conduct many zoom meetings with clients that are in states other than Florida or are outside the U.S. Our goal is to visit our clients personally at least two times a year.

We haven't really adjusted our frequency of communication for summer, it's business as usual. We know when our clients are on vacation and respect their time by not bothering them unless it's something urgent.

Since things slow down just a bit during the summer months, that gives us time to focus on our interns. Our goal is to create future advocates.

We also focus on making connections and prospecting on LinkedIn. I am always open to meeting new people and attracting opportunities that turn into new business. Over the summer I enjoy all the gatherings, vacations, and parties that clients and friends host. There I often meet new people who become clients over time.

Helen Andreoli, managing partner, Great Diamond Partners, Portland, Maine

Our beautiful state of Maine is called "Vacationland" because it's such an ideal place to visit and in the summer, clients flock to Maine. The most successful event we ever did, which clients still talk about, was a sunset cruise around Casco Bay during which we provided some lovely beverages and, most importantly, a big pile of lobster rolls!

One creative thing we did for a client was provide beach towels with the names of all 12 of her grandchildren. When they visit for the summer, they have their own special towel to take down to the beach. For the client, I believe she saw from that gesture that we are paying attention, not just to her finances, but to her family relationships, which are the most important thing to her.

More generally, we've found that clients, particularly in Maine, want to enjoy the summer with their families so we tend to have client review meetings more regularly in the spring and fall. Our communication strategies (newsletters, market commentary, and check-in calls) don't change at all in the summer months.

In the winter, our clients generally head south to escape the cold. During those frigid months is when we do our client travel visiting many clients, mostly in Florida. In person meetings are important as there is so much to be gained from sitting together in a family's living room or around a dining table.

Matt Cavanaugh, managing partner and director of investments, XXI Wealth, Chicago, Ill.

Every client is a little different. We try to be respectful of important family and vacation time and adapt to each client's needs and communication styles.

For some clients, summer is a time to unplug and relax. For others, such as some professional golfers and baseball players, it is right in the height of their busy season.

Every client is unique, and we are thoughtful to adapt to what works best for them. In that sense, in summer, it is business as usual.

Sally Cates is a managing director and is responsible for public relations and communications for Dynasty Financial Partners and the Dynasty Network of Advisors. Previously, she led global communications at Citi Private Bank and Smith Barney.

Editor's note: Guest commentaries like this one are written by authors outside the Barron's Advisor newsroom. They reflect the perspective and opinions of the authors. Submit feedback and commentary pitches to advisor.editors@barrons.com.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: CleanGo Innovations Inc. - Establishes a Strategic Joint Venture Company, CleanGo Arabia Ltd. In Partnership with Sanad Industrial Co in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia to Introduce Green Solutions for the Oil & Gas Industry
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

    VANCOUVER, BC & RIYADH, KSA –August 8th, 2025 –TheNewswire – CleanGoInnovations Inc. (CSE: CGII; OTCQB: CLGOF; FRA: APO), a leader inproprietary certified green cleaning and industrial solutions, isproud to announce the establishment of CleanGo Arabia LTD, a new jointventure in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. This significant initiative isa partnership with SANAD INDUSTRIAL CO LTD, a company under theesteemed EROG Holdings, a prominent Saudi-owned entity dedicated toserving the Kingdom's energy market.

    This milestone venture supports the Kingdom’scommitment to Saudi Vision 2030, aiming to localize advancedindustrial capabilities, diversify the economy, and establish SaudiArabia as a global hub for innovation and sustainability in the energysector.

    Supporting Localization Through GreenManufacturing

    CleanGo Arabia LTD will establish a state-of-the-artmanufacturing facility within Saudi Arabia, dedicated to the localproduction and distribution of CleanGo’s certified green solutions.The facility will enable high-performance, environmentally responsiblechemical products tailored for the oil services industry, not only inSaudi Arabia but across the GCC and MENA regions.

    By localizing the supply chain, the joint venture willsupport domestic job creation, knowledge transfer, and the growth ofSaudi industrial capabilities—all in direct alignment with Vision2030’s goals of building a resilient and self-sustaining industrialecosystem.

    Pioneering Green Solutions in the Oil& Gas Sector

    CleanGo Innovations' commitment to environmentalstewardship is at the forefront of this venture. Our proprietaryproducts stand out as some of the only certified green solutionsavailable for the oil services industry. In a sector traditionallyreliant on harsh chemicals, CleanGo's offerings provide a safe,effective, and environmentally responsible alternative.

    Diverse Applications in the OilPatch:

    CleanGo's flagship product, CG-100, offers atleast eight distinct uses within the oil services industry, showcasingits versatility and efficacy:

    1. Through Reservoir (WaterfloodEnhancement): CG-100 acts as a surfactant,reducing interfacial tension and improving sweep efficiency inwaterflood operations. This encourages higher oil recovery, increasesultimate oil recovery noted on reserve reports, and monetizes thereservoir with higher production rates. It breaks the oil's bondto the rock’s face, allowing it to enter the injected water stream,forming an oil bank as the chemical scours the rock’s face. Thisapplication generally involves a combination of a one-time batchtreatment and then continuous injection, requiring no mechanicalchanges to existing process piping or wellbore, nor regulatoryapprovals for its introduction.

    2. Wellbore Tubular Cleaning (WaxTreatments): CG-100 effectively treats wax andasphaltene deposition in wellbores, on artificial lift systems, and inflowlines. A designed volume of CG-100 is blended with treating fluid (produced water or 3-5% KCL water) anddisplaced down the tubing string for an overnight soak. Theapplication of heat lightens wax deposition on rod strings, reducingelectrical/fuel gas consumption. CG-100 also provides a residualcoating effect, deferring wax precipitation and promoting lowerelectrical consumption and lower system pressures.

    3. Historical Perforation/Frac PortCleaning: CG-100 is applied to dissolve pluggingin perforations and frac ports caused by wax precipitation. Thetreating chemical is squeezed into the reservoir with surface-appliedpressure and allowed to soak for 24-72 hours (typically 48 hours).This technique often requires a hot oiler to bring the fluid totemperature, which loosens the plugging. The emulsification propertiesof CG-100 then address the damage. This method does not require a rigto move tubulars and can also be used in conjunction with 15-28%by-volume hydrochloric acid.

    4. Flowline Cleaning (Pigging and FlowlineMaintenance): CG-100 makes wax brittle,facilitating its removal during pipeline pigging processes. It can beinjected between pigs in a batch mix or continuously injected with achemical pump to prevent accumulation. By removing partial plugging,CG-100 reduces backpressure on the system, lessening strain onartificial lift systems, lowering operational costs, and promotingmore fluid inflow from the reservoir. As a surfactant additive, nofacility retrofitting or overhauling of production tubulars isrequired.

    5. Facility Cleaning (Turnarounds andMaintenance): CG-100 reduces the viscosity ofsludge accumulations at the bottom of production vessels duringfacility turnarounds, which are typically conducted every 3-5 years.It can be sprayed onto tank walls with heated water and a gamma-jet toassist with cleaning and inspection. CG-100 has successfully cleanedtanks with dense oil that could not be cleaned by traditional methods,utilizing gamma jetting, vapor phase steaming, and pressurewashing.

    6. Completion Systems: Frac Spearheading/AcidEnhancement: CG-100 augments acid dissolving andwashing characteristics during fracture stimulations, reducing theparting pressure required to induce a fracture. This saves operatorsmoney on horsepower charges and reduces the chances of failed fractureplacement. It accelerates and improves the acid reactivity processrelative to standalone acid.

    7. Diluent Replacement for WellAbandonments: During well abandonments, CG-100helps remove oil from the production casing inside diameter, reducingthe risk of groundwater contamination and leaks through the ventedsurface cap. A dual treatment can be incorporated to minimize surfaceenvironmental contamination.

    By replacing conventional, often toxic, chemicals withour eco-friendly formulations, CleanGo Arabia LTD will enablecompanies to significantly reduce their environmental footprint whilemaintaining or even improving operational efficiency and safety. Thisaddresses critical industry needs for sustainable practices, wastereduction, and compliance with increasingly stringent environmentalregulations.

    Strategic Partnership with EROGHoldings and SANAD INDUSTRIAL CO LTD

    The collaboration with SANAD INDUSTRIAL CO LTD,controlled by EROG Holdings, is a cornerstone of this strategicexpansion. EROG (Earth Reservoirs for Oil and Gas), established in2014, has a proven track record as a comprehensive service providerfor the energy market in Saudi Arabia. EROG's vision is "Tobecome KSA’s consolidated source of supply for the oil & gasindustry including chemical work, freight and beyond, as we provideservices to foster a nurturing environment where compelling businessopportunities attract entrepreneurs from around the world, we produceand manufacture locally, ensuring satisfaction and the generation oflasting value for our nation1." Their deep understanding of the localand regional energy sector, coupled with their extensive network and logistical capabilities, will beinvaluable in the distribution and market penetration ofCleanGo's products within the oil services industry.

    The partnership will leverage EROG's expertise inproviding turnkey solutions and maintaining high-quality standardswith competitive and cost-effective prices, aligning perfectly withCleanGo’s mission to deliver superior, sustainable solutions.

    “This joint venture marks a pivotal moment forCleanGo Innovations,” stated Anthony Sarvucci, CEO of CleanGo Innovations Inc. “Partnering with EROG Holdings and Sanad Industrial Co.Ltd. provides us with unparalleled access to the rapidly growing oiland gas market in Saudi Arabia and the broader GCC and Africanregions. Their deep-rooted expertise and commitment to localmanufacturing align perfectly with our vision of delivering certifiedgreen solutions that not only enhance operational performance but alsochampion environmental responsibility.”

    Ali AlKaabi, CEO of SANAD INDUSTRIALCO LTD, added, “We are thrilled to join forceswith CleanGo Innovations. Their certified green products offer aunique and highly sought-after solution for the oil services industry,which is increasingly focused on sustainability. Through CleanGoArabia LTD, we will combine our regional market knowledge anddistribution strength with CleanGo’s innovative technology to createsignificant value for our customers and contribute to the Kingdom’sVision 2030 objectives of localization while fostering Saudi Madeinnovation.”

    This joint venture underscores CleanGo Innovations’commitment to global expansion and its dedication to fostering acleaner, greener future for critical industries worldwide.

    About CleanGo Innovations Inc.

    CleanGo Innovations Inc. (CSE: CGII; OTCQB: CLGOF; FRA:APO) is a leading developer and manufacturer of proprietary, certifiedgreen, non-toxic, and sustainable cleaning and industrial solutions.With a focus on innovation and environmental responsibility, CleanGooffers a diverse portfolio of products for retail, commercial, andindustrial applications, including the demanding oil and gas servicessector.

    For more information, visitwww.cleangoinnovations.com.

    About EROG Holdings

    Earth Reservoirs for Oil and Gas (EROG) is a 100%Saudi-owned company established in 2014. EROG strives to be acomprehensive service provider for the energy market industry in SaudiArabia, offering turnkey solutions and services for the oil & gasindustry, including chemical work, freight, and beyond. EROG iscommitted to local manufacturing and contributes to the Kingdom'seconomic growth and diversification.

    For more information, visit https://erogholding.com/.

    Contact:

    CleanGo Innovations Inc.

    Anthony Sarvucci

    President/CEO

    info@cleangogreengo.com

    346 202 6202

    SANAD INDUSTRIAL CO LTD

    Ali AlKaabi

    President/ CEO

    Forward-looking Information

    This news release may contain forward-lookingstatements and forward-looking information (collectively,“forward-looking statements”) within the meaning of applicableCanadian and U.S. securities legislation. Forward-looking statementscan often be identified by words such as “will”, “plans”,“expects”, “may”, “intends”, “anticipates”,“believes”, proposes” or variations of such words includingnegative variations thereof and phrases that refer to certain actions,events or results that may, could, would, might or will occur or betaken or achieved. Actual results could differ from those projected inany forward-looking statements due to numerous factors, includingrisks and uncertainties relating to general economic, market orbusiness conditions or changes in laws, regulations and policiesaffecting the Company’s operations. Investors are cautioned thatforward-looking statements are not guarantees of future performance orevents and, accordingly are cautioned not to put undue reliance onforward-looking statements due to the inherent uncertainty of suchstatements. Any forward-looking statements included in this newsrelease are made as of the date hereof and the Company does not intendto update or revise any forward-looking statements, except asexpressly required by law.

data.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC Initiates an Investigation into Allegations Against The Trade Desk, Inc. (TTD) And Encourages Investors to Reach Out
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / August 8, 2025 / Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is investigating potential claims on behalf of purchasers of The Trade Desk, Inc. ("Trade Desk" or "the Company") . Investors who purchased Trade Desk securities are encouraged to obtain additional information and assist the investigation by visiting the firm's site: bgandg.com/TTD.

Investigation Details

On August 7, 2025, Trade Desk reported its financial results for Q2 2025. Although the Company reported in line with expectations, it also announced it expects revenue growth to slow down in the third quarter. Based on this news, shares of Trade Desk fell by almost 40% on the next day.

What's Next?

If you are aware of any facts relating to this investigation or purchased Trade Desk securities, you can assist this investigation by visiting the firm's site: bgandg.com/TTD. You can also contact Peretz Bronstein or his client relations manager, Nathan Miller, of Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC:

There is No Cost to You

We represent investors in class actions on a contingency fee basis. That means we will ask the court to reimburse us for out-of-pocket expenses and attorneys' fees, usually a percentage of the total recovery, only if we are successful.

Why Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman

Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is a nationally recognized firm that represents investors in securities fraud class actions and shareholder derivative suits. Our firm has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors nationwide.

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn, X, Facebook, or Instagram.

Attorney advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

Contact

Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC

Peretz Bronstein or Nathan Miller

data.title: Urals diffs unchanged, Azeri BTC oil exports set to drop in Sept
data.description:
Urals crude differentials to dated Brent were unchanged on Friday in quiet trade, as sellers and buyers didn't rush to trade amid turbulent geopolitical situation, traders said.

India's biggest state refiners Indian Oil Corp and Bharat Petroleum have bought at least 22 million barrels of non-Russian crude for delivery in September and October, trade sources said, after the U.S. pressured India to halt purchases from Russia.

September volumes of Russian oil were yet to start trading, according to market participants, and sellers still had time to agree on the price level with buyers.

Azeri BTC crude oil exports from Turkey's Ceyhan port are set at 16.5 million barrels for September, down from 17.3 million barrels in August, the schedule showed on Friday.

PLATTS WINDOW

  • No bids or offers were made for Urals, Azeri BTC or CPC Blend in the Platts window on Friday.

NEWS

  • Reliance Industries is likely to shift back to its traditional Middle Eastern sources for oil if India yields to pressure from U.S. President Donald Trump to cut Russian imports, trade sources said.

data.title: Government Preparing to IPO Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac Later This Year, Sources Say — WSJ
data.description:
By Corrie Driebusch, AnnaMaria Andriotis and Gina Heeb

The Trump administration is preparing to sell stock in mortgage giants Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac in an offering it believes could raise around $30 billion and kick off later this year, according to people familiar with the matter.

The plans being discussed by some administration officials could value the companies at roughly $500 billion or more combined and involve selling between 5% and 15% of their stock, some of the people said. Still up for debate is whether the mortgage giants would IPO as one company or two separate entities.

Fannie and Freddie, which bundle and sell mortgages, have been under government control since the 2008 financial crisis and rely on a government-backed guarantee to protect investors from losses.

It's unclear whether Fannie and Freddie will remain under government conservatorship. Bill Pulte, head of the Federal Housing Finance Agency, has in the past suggested the firms could remain under conservatorship while conducting a share offering, without clarifying how it would work.

The CEOs of six large banks — Morgan Stanley, JPMorgan Chase, Goldman Sachs, Citigroup, Wells Fargo and Bank of America — traveled to D.C. in recent weeks to meet with President Trump to discuss potential plans for Fannie and Freddie .

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick and FHFA's Pulte were also present at several of the meetings, people familiar with the matter said.

Trump allies began laying the groundwork to pursue public offerings for Fannie and Freddie months before he was re-elected, The Wall Street Journal reported last year.

Proponents have long said that such a transaction — which at the values being discussed would rank among the largest stock offerings in history — could reduce the country's deficit and return money to taxpayers.

There is no guarantee a deal will come together. Initial public offerings take time to prepare, and one as complicated as offering shares of Fannie and Freddie is a large undertaking. Some bankers are skeptical whether such a quick timeline is likely. Previous attempts to privatize the firms, including by Trump in his first term, failed to gain traction.

Fannie and Freddie have long benefited from the market expectation that the government would bail them out of any trouble, through a so-called implicit guarantee. Analysts have warned that mortgage rates could rise without that government support.

President Trump has said he wants the firms to keep the guarantee in the case of a public offering but hasn't elaborated on how that might work. Any explicit guarantee would likely raise legislative and accounting challenges.

The Treasury Department holds warrants to purchase about 80% of Fannie and Freddie common stock and also holds senior preferred shares. Other investors own junior preferred shares or common stock. The government would be selling stock in the offering.

Write to Corrie Driebusch at corrie.driebusch@wsj.com, AnnaMaria Andriotis at annamaria.andriotis@wsj.com and Gina Heeb at gina.heeb@wsj.com

data.title: Oil Futures End Volatile Week Lower — Market Talk
data.description: Oil futures end the session near flat but with sharp weekly losses as the market digests the OPEC+ decision to raise production by another 547,000 barrels a day in September and worries about the impact of U.S. import tariffs on global growth. U.S. demands that India stop buying Russian oil or pay higher tariffs grabs attention, but doesn't lift prices. "If Russian flows are curtailed it would be a bullish aspect to prices. If, however, the current flows remain, the traders' focus will move back to OPEC+ increasing global supplies and a weaker seasonal demand that normally appears in the August through November time frame," Dennis Kissler of BOK Financial says in a note. WTI is unchanged at $63.88 a barrel and Brent inches up 0.2% to $66.59, for weekly losses of 5.1% and 4.4%, respectively. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Energy Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy markets. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1513 ET - Oil futures end the session near flat but with sharp weekly losses as the market digests the OPEC+ decision to raise production by another 547,000 barrels a day in September and worries about the impact of U.S. import tariffs on global growth. U.S. demands that India stop buying Russian oil or pay higher tariffs grabs attention, but doesn't lift prices. "If Russian flows are curtailed it would be a bullish aspect to prices. If, however, the current flows remain, the traders' focus will move back to OPEC+ increasing global supplies and a weaker seasonal demand that normally appears in the August through November time frame," Dennis Kissler of BOK Financial says in a note. WTI is unchanged at $63.88 a barrel and Brent inches up 0.2% to $66.59, for weekly losses of 5.1% and 4.4%, respectively. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1345 ET [Dow Jones]--The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. edged up by one this week to 411, ending a string of 14 weeks of declines, oil service firm Baker Hughes reports. Oil rigs were down by 74 from a year ago. Natural-gas rigs slipped by one to 123 after adding 16 in the previous three weeks. Bank of America strategists Francisco Blanch and Clifton White attribute the climb in natural-gas rigs, led by the Haynesville region, partly to optimism about demand for LNG exports. "It only took 2026 gas prices trading $4-$4.5/mmBtu year to date to increase the natural-gas rig count by more than 20% since 1Q25," they said in a note. "We expect to see incremental production from these newly added gas rigs just as Golden Pass [LNG] enters service by the end of the year." (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1117 ET - Russian exports of oil and condensates have stayed relatively high at 4.3 million barrels a day in 1H25, down from 4.8 million b/d in 2024, although the destination in recent years has shifted more to Asia, the EIA says in an analysis as the oil market ponders the future of India's purchases. Europe's share of Russian exports fell to 11% in 1H25 from 51% in 2020, while Asia and Oceania's share rose to 81% last year from 41% in 2020. Exports from Russia to India increased from about 50,000 b/d in 2020 to 1.7 million b/d in 2024 and averaged 1.6 million b/d in 1H25, the EIA says. China remains the biggest buyer at 2 million b/d in 1H25. Oil futures are down for a seventh straight session in choppy trade, with WTI off 0.4% at $63.62 and Brent down 0.3% at $66.23. (anthon.harrup@wsj.com)

0935 ET - U.S. natural gas futures are little changed, struggling to gain traction despite this week's small inventory build and jump in LNG feedgas flows. "With storage surpluses to challenge 200 Bcf above five-year norms next week and the heart of hurricane season looming, natural gas may retest support after next week's heat wave fades and it settles into a new late-summer trading range," Eli Rubin of EBW Analytics says in a note. "While daily cooling demand will continue higher into early next week, forecasts have receded across the Gulf Coast and Southeast." Nymex natural gas is off 0.3% at $3.057/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0908 ET - Oil futures pick up cautiously while on track for steep weekly losses after six straight sessions in the red. Much trading this week has focused on India's response to U.S. tariffs over its purchases of Russian oil, and to what extent India will cut back on those purchases. Plans for a Trump-Putin meeting have also taken off some of the upside. "Flat price and spreads are sending surprisingly bearish signals," Neil Crosby of Sparta Commodities says in a note. "It does seem like spreads should be reacting to evidence that various Indian refiners are fairly active on the spot crude market, both light and medium quality and from a variety of origins." WTI is up 0.4% at $64.16 and Brent is up 0.5% at $66.78.(anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0535 ET - The near-term outlook for China's steel industry remains uncertain despite improved sentiment, according to HSBC economists in a research note. China's steel exports remained strong in July, backed by competitive China prices, they say. Beijing's recent campaign aiming to reduce excess capability also improved overall market sentiment, they say. However, HSBC believes recent catalysts support sentiment but not enough to rebalance supply and demand dynamics. "We are cautious near term and focused on implementation timelines and underlying fundamentals," they note. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0416 ET - The Swedish krona is better positioned compared to the Norwegian krone if geopolitical risk related to the Russia-Ukraine conflict are priced out, ING's Francesco Pesole says in a note. That is due to the two currencies' opposite exposures to energy prices with Norway a major oil and gas producer, he says. This probably explains the Swedish krona's recent rise against the Norwegian krone after news of President Trump meeting with his Russian counterpart Vladimir Putin next week. The Norwegian krone rises 0.1% to 0.9354 Swedish kronor, having reached a two-and-a-half-week low of 0.9338 earlier, LSEG data show. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0130 ET - Sunonwealth Electric Machine Industry's 2H outlook appears strong, driven by telecom, server, IT and industrial segments, Daiwa Capital Markets analysts say in a research report. The Taiwanese fan and motor provider's industrial revenue jumped 45% on year in 1H on increased AI power supply. The company expects its 3Q revenue to grow by 5%-10% on quarter, mainly led by AI server and other segments, the analysts note. The brokerage lifts its 2025-2027 EPS forecasts for the company by 15%-18% to reflect its higher revenue and margin assumptions. It raises the stock's target price to NT$152.00 from NT$115.00 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 7.4% higher at NT$131.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Akamai Technologies Is Maintained at Sector Outperform by Scotiabank
RIVERVIEW BANCORP INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Riverview Bancorp Inc., a regional bank operating primarily in Washington and Oregon, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational activities, showcasing growth in key financial metrics and a stable business outlook.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest and Dividend Income: $15.375 million, reflecting an increase from the previous year due to higher interest and fees on loans receivable.
  • Net Interest Income: $9.841 million, showing growth compared to the prior year, driven by increased interest income and stable interest expenses.
  • Net Income: $1.225 million, an improvement over the previous year's $966 thousand, indicating better profitability.
  • Basic EPS: $0.06, up from $0.05 in the previous year, reflecting improved earnings per share.
  • Diluted EPS: $0.06, consistent with Basic EPS, showing no dilution effect.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported non-interest income from various segments, including:
    • Asset management fees: $1,552,000
    • Debit card and ATM fees: $762,000
    • Deposit-related fees: $528,000
    • Loan-related fees: $110,000
  • Geographical Performance: The company's business activities are primarily concentrated in the states of Washington and Oregon, indicating a regional focus in these areas. This concentration suggests that the company is leveraging its regional presence to drive business operations.
  • Future Outlook: The company expects the unaudited consolidated results of operations for the three months ended June 30, 2025, to not necessarily be indicative of the results which may be expected for the entire fiscal year ending March 31, 2026. This suggests a cautious outlook on future performance, considering potential variability in operational results.

SEC Filing: RIVERVIEW BANCORP INC [ RVSB ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: CVB FINANCIAL CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
CVB Financial Corp, a prominent financial institution operating through its subsidiary Citizens Business Bank, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights, reflecting its strategic focus on traditional banking activities and its robust presence in California.

Financial Highlights

For the three months ended June 30, 2025, CVB Financial Corp reported the following key financial metrics:

  • Total Interest Income: $144.2 million, a decrease from $159.1 million in the same period in 2024, primarily due to lower interest income from loans and leases.
  • Net Interest Income: $111.6 million, slightly up from $110.8 million in the same period in 2024, indicating stable interest margins despite a decrease in total interest income.
  • Net Earnings: $50.6 million, compared to $50.0 million in the same period in 2024, showing a slight increase in profitability.
  • Basic Earnings Per Common Share: $0.37, compared to $0.36 in the same period in 2024, reflecting a modest improvement in earnings per share.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Common Share: $0.37, consistent with the basic earnings per share, indicating no significant dilution effect from potential share conversions.

Business Highlights

CVB Financial Corp's business performance for the second quarter of 2025 includes the following highlights:

  • Business Segments: The company operates as a single reportable segment, focusing on traditional banking activities through its subsidiary, Citizens Business Bank. The bank provides a range of products and services, including loans for commercial businesses, commercial real estate, multi-family, construction, land, dairy & livestock, agribusiness, consumer, and government-guaranteed small business loans. Additionally, the bank offers business deposit products and treasury cash management services.
  • Geographical Performance: The company's operations are concentrated in California, where it serves small to mid-sized businesses and individuals. As of June 30, 2025, the bank operated 62 banking centers and three trust office locations across the state.
  • Loan Portfolio Composition: As of June 30, 2025, 81.64% of the company's total loan portfolio consisted of real estate loans, with commercial real estate loans representing 78.0% of total loans. The real estate loans and construction loans are primarily secured by properties located in California.
  • Credit Quality and Risk Management: The company employs a loan risk rating system to monitor credit quality, categorizing loans into Pass, Special Mention, Substandard, Doubtful, and Loss. This system helps in assessing the adequacy of the allowance for credit losses and managing credit risk effectively.
  • Allowance for Credit Losses: The allowance for credit losses (ACL) was $78.0 million as of June 30, 2025, representing 0.93% of total loans and leases at amortized cost. The ACL is based on lifetime loss rate models and considers various risk attributes and macroeconomic variables.
  • Future Outlook: The company's economic forecast anticipates a decline in Real GDP in the second half of 2025, with growth expected to remain below 1% until the second half of 2026. The unemployment rate is projected to reach 5% by early 2026 and remain elevated through 2028. Management believes the ACL is appropriate given current economic conditions, but acknowledges potential future provisions due to inflationary pressures, high interest rates, and geopolitical events.

SEC Filing: CVB FINANCIAL CORP [ CVBF ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: CBOT wheat ends down on plentiful global supply
data.description:
U.S. wheat futures lost an export-fuelled rebound on Friday as abundant global supplies of the grain and technical trading weighed on prices.

  • Northern Hemisphere wheat harvests have been flooding the market with new-crop grain.

  • Cheap prices have, however, stimulated demand. Weekly U.S. export sales reported by the U.S. Department of Agriculture on Thursday showed wheat volumes above analyst estimates.

  • Consultancy IKAR said on Friday it had raised its 2025 Russian wheat crop forecast to 84.5 million metric tons from 84.0 million previously, supporting expectations that harvest results were improving in the world's biggest wheat exporter after disappointing early reports.

  • Farmers in Argentina have sown a larger wheat area this year than last and early crop conditions are highly favourable, the Buenos Aires Grains Exchange said.

  • CBOT September soft red winter wheat (WU25) fell 3-3/4 cents to $5.14-1/2 a bushel.

  • K.C. September hard red winter wheat (KWU25) settled 3-1/4 cents lower to $5.18-1/4 a bushel.

  • Minneapolis September spring wheat (MWEU25) settled 2-3/4 cents lower at $5.76-3/4 a bushel.

data.title: Tethys Petroleum Announces Annual General Meeting Resolution Update
data.description:
Grand Cayman, Cayman Islands--(Newsfile Corp. - August 8, 2025) - Tethys Petroleum Limited ("Tethys" or the "Company") announces that it has been provided with notices of intention to nominate directors of the Company at the annual general meeting of shareholders to be held on August 11, 2025.

Fincraft Group LLP has given notice of its intention to nominate Askar Ismailov as a director. Gazexport Limited has given notice of its intention to nominate Paul J. Ostling and Piers Johnson as directors.

Fincraft has not provided the Company with biographical information in respect of Mr. Ismailov. Gazexport has provided to Company with biographical information in respect of Mssrs. Ostling and Johnson, which is generally summarized as follows:

  • Paul J. Ostling served as an attorney for Ernst and Young for thirty years handling complex regulatory and corporate matters. Paul has served on a number of boards of directors, run several companies, and developed his own entrepreneurial pursuits. He has particular expertise in large telecoms, industrial mining chemicals and complex global distribution enterprises. Current Board memberships and operating roles include CinemaLab, PISMOS Technology International Inc., Business Council for International Understanding, and Fordham University School of Law.
  • Piers Johnson extensively trained in the Petroleum Business after working for Flopetrol Johnston Schlumberger. Piers started Oilfield Production Consultants Limited, a Petroleum and Reservoir Engineering Consultancy specializing in Operational Procedures, Integrated Studies, Wellsite Supervision and Well Test Analysis.

Management of Tethys has not undertaken any independent review of the credentials of such nominees and consideration of such nominees will be addressed at the upcoming shareholders meeting.

About Tethys

Tethys is focused on oil and gas exploration and production activities in Central Asia and the Caspian Region.

Disclaimer

Some of the statements in this document are forward-looking. No part of this announcement constitutes, or shall be taken to constitute, an invitation or inducement to invest in the Company or any other entity and shareholders of the Company are cautioned not to place undue reliance on the forward-looking statements. Save as required by applicable law, the Company does not undertake to update or change any forward-looking statements to reflect events occurring after the date of this announcement.

Neither the TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

Contact Information:

Tethys Petroleum

Casey McCandless

Chief Financial Officer 

901-763-4001

info@tethys-group.com

www.tethys-group.com

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/261881

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 3 PM ET: U.S. Stocks Rise as Gold Hits New High | Gold ...
data.description:
U.S. Stocks Rise as Gold Hits New High

U.S stocks rose at the end of a week heavy on tariff updates. The gold market, meanwhile, was rattled by unexpected news tariffs will apply to gold bars.

----

Gold Futures Hit Record High as U.S. Import Tariff Sparks Turmoil

Gold climbed to a fresh record high after a U.S. government agency clarified that import tariffs would apply to one-kilo gold bars, sparking turmoil in the global bullion market.

----

Trump Reminds CEOs Who the Ultimate Boss Is

The president who ran on his business acumen isn't holding back in telling corporate bosses how to run their companies-or when to resign.

----

Municipal Bonds May Not Remain This Cheap For Long

A steady increase in municipal-bond supply, coupled with the survival of their tax benefit in President Trump's spending bill, is making the securities more attractive.

----

China's Car Sales Growth Slows as Price War Cools

Retail sales of passenger cars rose 6.3% in July from a year earlier to 1.8 million vehicles, data from the China Passenger Car Association showed Friday.

----

Week Ahead for FX, Bonds: U.S. Inflation, China Activity Data in Focus

U.S. inflation data for July will be closely watched, particularly after recent weak jobs data increased prospects for U.S. interest-rate cuts, while retail sales will also give a clue about how tariffs are affecting consumers.

----

Canada Jobless Rate Steadies in July Even as Employment Pulls Back

Canada's labor market fell back to earth with a sharp drop in employment in July, putting a spotlight on the challenges the country's job seekers continue to face.

----

Irish Factory Output Slumped in June as Exports to U.S. Fell

Exports to the U.S. halved, helping to push Ireland's economy into contraction after a first-quarter boom.

----

In Latest Tariff Onslaught, China Isn't a Target. Here's Why.

The administration seems determined to secure a meeting between President Donald Trump and China's Xi Jinping this fall in hopes of securing a deal on trade and other fronts.

----

Trump Will Nominate Economic Adviser Stephen Miran to Fed Vacancy

Miran is currently the head of the White House Council of Economic Advisers.

----

AI Spending Is Propping Up the Economy, Right? It's Complicated.

The AI spending bonanza has divided economists' opinion.

----

The Corporate Bond Market's Utter Faith in the U.S. Economy

U.S. companies can now unload their debt at the lowest rates so far this year.

----

The 30-Year Bond Auction Went Poorly. Yields Are Popping Higher.

An auction of government bonds that expires in three decades saw weak demand on Thursday, in an ominous sign for the Treasury market.

----

How Quantum Computing Could Upend Bitcoin

Hackers stand to gain "a superpower." Will the crypto industry be ready?

data.title: Dow Jones Top Company Headlines at 3 PM ET: Heartflow Stock Opens Nearly 50% Above IPO Price in Latest Hot Debut | Trump ...
data.description:
Heartflow Stock Opens Nearly 50% Above IPO Price in Latest Hot Debut

Medical imaging company Heartflow rose nearly 50% on its first day, joining Firefly, Figma, and Circle as big IPO gainers.

----

Trump Preparing IPO for Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac Later This Year

Some officials believe the offering could raise around $30 billion and value the combined firms at roughly $500 billion or more.

----

Wendy's Cuts Outlook as U.S. Sales Falter

Wendy's lowered its full-year earnings and sales guidance after reporting declining revenue in the second quarter tied to weakness among its U.S. restaurants.

----

Lyft Bets on Robotaxis to Drive Revenue Growth

Lyft is betting on a revenue boost from deploying autonomous vehicles in the U.S. and Europe as the San Francisco-based ride-sharing company works to scale its robotaxi presence.

----

Under Armour Expects Struggles to Continue as Tariffs Dent Results

The sportswear company reported a drop in first-quarter profit and guided for steeper declines in the current quarter. It also said it expects tariff headwinds to cut its fiscal-year profit in half.

----

How Intel's CEO Helped Create China's Chip Industry

Lip-Bu Tan's venture-capital firm has supported some of the biggest names in China's chip industry. With U.S.-China tensions running high, those investments no longer look so benign.

----

Boeing's $4.7 Billion Spirit AeroSystems Deal Cleared in U.K.

Officials said they wouldn't open an in-depth probe into Boeing's deal to acquire the fuselage maker, effectively clearing the transaction.

----

AE Industrial's Bet on Rocket Maker Firefly Soars in IPO

The firm's stake in the space company climbed to around $3.5 billion as Firefly shares surge.

----

Chip Maker SMIC Sees Limited Impact of Trump's Tariffs

China's largest chip maker said the U.S. tariffs should continue to have a limited impact as demand from domestic clients is increasing sharply and overseas clients are better prepared for the headwinds.

----

Meta, Robby Starbuck Settle AI Defamation Lawsuit

The conservative activist will advise the company on removing political bias from its AI tools.

----

Jaguar Land Rover Backs Guidance Despite Hit From Tariffs and EV Transition

Revenue fell 9.2% and reflected the U.S. putting a 25% tariff on top of an existing 2.5% duty on U.K. and EU-made vehicles exports.

----

Battle for Seattle: Can Delta Keep Dominating International Flights?

Alaska Airlines is launching flights to Europe and Asia, bringing more competition to the global gateway.

----

Why a Weight-Loss Pill Is Still a Big Deal

Eli Lilly shares fell after the company's oral drug showed less weight loss than hoped. It could still be huge.

----

Apple Got the Jump on Tariffs, Deciding Years Ago to Make iPhones in India

CEO Tim Cook sought new factory locales outside China, a prescient move that along with a new $100 billion U.S. investment will shield the company-for now.

----

Jamie Dimon Took a Bus Tour Down South. We Rode Along.

Top executives at JPMorgan Chase traveled through states including Mississippi and Alabama, where the bank is expanding its reach.

----

Munich Re Lowers Revenue View on Currency Impacts, Shares Tumble

The company cut its insurance revenue guidance for the year due to business and exchange-rate developments, but backed its profit view.

data.title: Occidental Unloads $950M in Assets--Debt Drop Could Signal Major Comeback
data.description:
Occidental is quietly making big moves. Since April, the company has inked four separate deals to offload select Permian Basin assetspulling in around $950 million. That cash is earmarked for one thing: chopping down debt. Among the transactions, a $580 million sale to an affiliate of Enterprise Products Partners targets gas gathering assets in the Midland Basin. The deal is still pending regulatory green lights but could be finalized soon. The rest came from smaller, non-core and non-operated Permian assets no longer in the company's immediate plans.

This is all part of Occidental's broader post-CrownRock cleanup. Since July 2024, it has repaid roughly $7.5 billion in debta figure that could jump even higher once the $580 million sale closes. That brings total divestitures since the CrownRock acquisition to nearly $4 billion. The pace and scale of deleveraging suggest a company that's not just managing its balance sheetbut reshaping it entirely.

CEO Vicki Hollub is playing a long game. She believes the current asset base is the strongest in Occidental's history and plans to keep trimming what's unnecessary. Her goal? A leaner, sharper portfolio that can generate sustainable value for shareholders. With core positions in the Permian, DJ Basin, and Gulf of Mexico still intact, Occidental could be positioning itself for stronger capital returns once the dust settles.

data.title: CBOT corn finishes lower amid abundant supply
data.description:
Chicago Board of Trade corn futures finished lower on Friday after spending the day chopping up and down as traders weighed an uptick in demand against a backdrop of abundant supply.

  • Expectations of plentiful supply have weighed heavily on prices as growing conditions for U.S. corn and soybeans appeared ideal.

  • However, lower prices have sparked an uptick in demand.

  • A series of flash sales of corn in recent days to Mexico, Guatemala and unknown destinations have helped support prices.

  • Short-covering also helped wheat and corn prices rebound as traders exited their positions ahead of an upcoming U.S. Department of Agriculture report.

  • U.S. exporters sold 125,000 metric tons of corn to unknown destinations for 2025/26 delivery, the U.S. Department of Agriculture reported.

  • The U.S. is expected to produce bumper corn harvests later in the year. Analysts polled by Reuters think the U.S. Department of Agriculture will raise its estimates in a monthly report due on August 12.

  • CBOT December contract (CZ25) fell 1-1/2 cents to close at $4.05-1/2 per bushel.

data.title: Inflation Data, China Tariff Deadline: What to Watch in the Coming Week — WSJ
data.description:
By Aaron Lucchetti

Much of the world received their latest tariff rates from President Trump this week, but next week brings another key deadline-for the extension of the U.S. trade truce with China. Earnings are coming from big companies including Deere, Cisco Systems and Applied Materials. Tuesday also brings consumer inflation data. The report will be closely followed by investors trying to pinpoint how many interest-rate cuts the Federal Reserve will make for the rest of 2025. Here's what to watch:

Saturday, Aug. 9

  • Fed speaker: Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman is due to speak at a bankers' meeting in Colorado. Bowman was one of two Fed governors to dissent from the central bank's decision last month to hold interest rates steady.

Monday, Aug. 11

  • Earnings: Onetime meme stock AMC Entertainment (AMC) reports after the close.

Tuesday, Aug. 12

  • Inflation report: July's consumer price index data is due at 8:30 a.m. ET. The core inflation reading, which excludes food and energy, is expected to come in at 3.1% year-over-year, slightly hotter than the prior month's 2.9%.
  • More data: The Treasury Department releases surplus and deficit figures for July, and the National Federation of Independent Business will disclose its index of small-business trends.
  • Tariff deadline: The U.S. and China have been discussing a possible three-month extension to an earlier trade truce that remains in place until Tuesday. See the latest on tariffs here.
  • Earnings: AI company CoreWeave (CRWV), crypto firm Circle Internet Group (CRCL).

Wednesday, Aug. 13

  • Earnings: Cisco Systems (CSCO), after market close.

Thursday, Aug. 14

  • Economic data: The producer price index for July gives another indication of inflation. Weekly jobless claims are also due.
  • Earnings: Deere & Co. (DE), before the market opens. Applied Materials (AMAT), after the close.

Friday, Aug. 15

  • Economic data: Retail sales data, a preliminary reading on consumer sentiment from the University of Michigan, and July data on export and import prices. Also, the New York Fed's Empire State manufacturing survey and nationwide industrial-production numbers are due.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Maximum Entertainment secures USD 4 million term loan commitment from financial partner
data.description:
Stockholm, Sweden (August 8, 2025) - Maximum Entertainment AB (Nasdaq: MAXENT B) today announces that the company has secured a delayed draw term loan commitment of USD 4.0 million. The delayed draw term loan has a maturity of twelve (12) months and carries customary covenants and collateral requirements.

The company has today made an initial drawdown of USD 2.75 million with the balance of USD 1.25 million available for drawdowns over the next six (6) months subject to senior creditor approval. The proceeds will primarily be used for cost of goods.
